goal: Force-fetch actor logs + notes ref so non-fast-forward rewrites don't strand phantom intents

summary.title: Force-fetch actor logs + notes ref so upstream rewrites do not strand phantom intents
summary.what: Adds `+` prefix to the actor-log and notes-ref refspecs in Sync so upstream non-fast-forward rewrites overwrite the local mirror. Without this, a force-pushed actor log from another actor (doctor-rebuild, rebase, or any other rewrite) would silently leave our mirror at a stale chain. scanMainNotes would then encounter notes referencing sealed events absent from that stale mirror and synthesise empty IntentView stubs — what users see as merged-status rows with no goal/actor/sealed_at metadata.
summary.why: User reported three log rows on the dogfood repo (int_be2a4e9f, int_0fa25e16, int_4e6bbd80) showing status=merged but every other field blank. Bisected to z2z23n0 having force-pushed their actor log; my fetch silently refused the non-fast-forward update. Manual `git fetch +refs/heads/...` showed the rewrite (`(forced update)`). Once forced, the IntentViews populated correctly. Auto-fix the underlying refspec so future similar rewrites just work.
summary.user_goal: Ship that fix before MVP rollout — empty rows look like data corruption to a new user and erode trust in mainline log being the source of truth.

decisions.point: Force-fetch actor logs vs require manual user intervention
decisions.rationale: Actor logs have a single owner — the actor pushing. Third parties have no local write authority over them. Force-overwriting the local mirror IS the right semantic; we are not racing against local writes.

Same single-source-of-truth argument: whoever last pinned/merged is authoritative. Local notes are written by Pin and pushed; we never accumulate local-only changes other than transient ones during a Pin run.

Main branch rewrites are a separate, scarier class of operation. Users resolve them via git directly. Force-fetching main inside mainline sync would silently overwrite work-in-progress checkouts; explicitly not in scope here.

rejected.alternative: Detect non-fast-forward and prompt the user
rejected.reason: Adds friction to the most common case (no rewrite) for a corner case. Force-fetch is correct by design for actor logs.

rejected.alternative: Suppress phantom IntentView stubs at scanMainNotes
rejected.reason: Would mask genuinely missing sealed events, which is also useful information. The fix should be at the fetch layer where the gap originated, not the rendering layer where it surfaced.

risks: A rogue actor force-pushing their actor log to remove unfavourable history would now silently overwrite my view. But the same is true if they push fresh history that omits old events — append-only is enforceable only by social convention since git allows force-push. This change does not weaken the integrity model; it just makes the read path consistent with the existing write/push model.

If a user ever did manually edit a refs/remotes/origin/_mainline/actor/* ref (extremely unusual; mainline never tells them to), the next sync silently overwrites it. Documented behaviour: those refs are meant to be a passive mirror, not a workspace.
followups: Consider showing a "metadata pending" marker in mainline log for IntentViews that lack sealed_event_id (the stub case) — even with this fix, a brand-new clone might briefly see notes before fetching the corresponding actor log fully. Cosmetic improvement; small.
Doctor check: detect IntentViews with missing sealed_event_id and prompt the user to retry sync (now force-fetch will resolve them).

architectural_claims: Actor logs and notes ref are owned by their writer; third-party clients force-fetch them so upstream rewrites are reflected immediately.
Main branch is intentionally NOT force-fetched — main rewrites are a separate user concern resolved via git directly.
behavioral_changes: Sync now force-fetches refs/heads/<prefix>/*  and refs/notes/mainline/* (was fast-forward-only).
Empty/phantom IntentView rows in `mainline log` caused by stale actor-log mirrors should self-resolve on next sync after this lands.
